    output ariane_axi::resp_t         axi_resp_o,

reset_facts.signal_or_condition: always_ff @(posedge clk_i or negedge rst_ni) begin
else begin $error("AXI Lite does not support bursts larger than 1 or byte length unequal to the native bus size"); $stop(); end
reset_facts.text_preview:
assert property (@(posedge clk_i) axi_req_i.ar_valid |->  ((axi_req_i.ar.len == 8'b0)))
    else begin $error("AXI Lite does not support bursts larger than 1 or byte length unequal to the native bus size"); $stop(); end
    // do the same for the write channel
    assert property (@(posedge clk_i) axi_req_i.aw_valid |->  ((axi_req_i.aw.len == 8'b0)))
    else begin $error("AXI Lite does not support bursts larger than 1 or byte length unequal to the native bus size"); $stop(); end
    `endif

reset_facts.signal_or_condition: else begin $error("AXI Lite does not support bursts larger than 1 or byte length unequal to the native bus size"); $stop(); end
reset_facts.text_preview:
assert property (@(posedge clk_i) axi_req_i.aw_valid |->  ((axi_req_i.aw.len == 8'b0)))
    else begin $error("AXI Lite does not support bursts larger than 1 or byte length unequal to the native bus size"); $stop(); end
    `endif

        .axi_resp_o ( axi_resp_o ),

        .en_o       ( en_acct    ),

       .rst_ni        ( rst_ni && ~rst_8         ),

       .source_addr_msb_i ( source_addr_msb_reg ),

csr_facts.text: enum logic [2:0] {Idle, WriteSecond, ReadSecond, WriteResp, ReadResp} state_d, state_q;
enum logic [1:0] { IDLE, READ, WRITE, WRITE_B } state_q, state_d;
for (int i = 0; i < PMP_LEN; i++) begin

asset_candidates.name: reglk_ctrl
asset_candidates.rationale: Global register-lock control vector likely mediates writes/configuration of peripherals.

asset_candidates.name: acc_ctrl
asset_candidates.rationale: Privilege-indexed access-control vector used to gate peripheral requests.

asset_candidates.name: acc_ctrl_c
asset_candidates.rationale: Combinational expansion/aliasing of access-control bits by privilege/peripheral index.

asset_candidates.name: debug capability outputs
asset_candidates.rationale: Debug module can request hart debug, non-debug reset, and debug active state.

asset_candidates.name: JTAG/DMI debug state
asset_candidates.rationale: JTAG authentication/unlock and DMI operation fields control debug access.

asset_candidates.name: debug module bus interface state
asset_candidates.rationale: Debug slave/master address and data channels can access or expose memory/register state.

asset_candidates.name: boot ROM contents/read data
asset_candidates.rationale: Boot ROM is a memory region whose read access is mediated by access-control logic.

asset_candidates.name: fuse region/data
asset_candidates.rationale: Fuse values are security-sensitive configuration/secret-like state.

asset_candidates.name: reset control outputs/peripheral reset state
asset_candidates.rationale: Reset controls can alter lifecycle/security state of peripherals.

asset_candidates.name: DMA configuration registers
asset_candidates.rationale: Software-visible DMA registers configure transfer source, destination, length, and status.

asset_candidates.name: DMA internal control/state registers
asset_candidates.rationale: DMA state determines whether memory-copy operations are launched and reported.

asset_candidates.name: DMA PMP check state
asset_candidates.rationale: DMA-specific PMP check state mediates access to protected memory regions.

asset_candidates.name: PMP configuration and match outputs
asset_candidates.rationale: Physical memory protection entries and allow/match outputs define access-control boundaries.

asset_candidates.name: CLINT timer/software interrupt registers path
asset_candidates.rationale: CLINT MMIO controls timer/IPI effects visible to harts and requires mediated access.

asset_candidates.name: crypto/security peripheral NoC endpoints
asset_candidates.rationale: Crypto/RNG peripheral responses may expose sensitive data or control security-sensitive accelerators.

subject_candidates.name: CPU/software via privilege level
subject_candidates.rationale: Requester identity/privilege is represented by current privilege level and RISC-V interface.

subject_candidates.name: JTAG external debugger
subject_candidates.rationale: External/debug test interface issues debug/DMI requests.

subject_candidates.name: DMI/debug module requester
subject_candidates.rationale: DMI channel carries debug read/write operations.

subject_candidates.name: Debug module bus master/slave
subject_candidates.rationale: Debug module participates as bus requester/target for memory/register accesses.

subject_candidates.name: NoC/bus masters targeting peripherals
subject_candidates.rationale: Incoming NoC request channels are subjects for MMIO/peripheral access.

subject_candidates.name: AXI-Lite requester for CLINT/DMA wrappers
subject_candidates.rationale: AXI interface carries software-visible read/write requests.

subject_candidates.name: DMA engine
subject_candidates.rationale: DMA becomes an autonomous requester for memory transfer operations once configured.

subject_candidates.name: test mode requester/control
subject_candidates.rationale: Test mode may alter mediation or expose debug/test access paths.

operation_candidates.name: peripheral request/read gating
operation_candidates.rationale: Boot ROM request is gated before read data is returned.

operation_candidates.name: configure/update access-control policy
operation_candidates.rationale: Access and lock vectors are policy state likely updated via register-lock/accounting peripheral paths.

operation_candidates.name: DMI debug operation selection
operation_candidates.rationale: DMI op field determines debug read/write/no-op behavior.

operation_candidates.name: JTAG unlock/authentication
operation_candidates.rationale: Unlock state/hash are involved in authorizing debug access.

operation_candidates.name: debug reset/request
operation_candidates.rationale: Debug path can trigger reset or enter debug mode.

operation_candidates.name: debug bus memory access
operation_candidates.rationale: Debug bus interfaces encode addressable read/write accesses.

operation_candidates.name: DMA register write/read
operation_candidates.rationale: AXI-mapped DMA registers are configured and read by software.

operation_candidates.name: DMA transfer trigger
operation_candidates.rationale: DMA configuration initiates transfer from source to destination.

operation_candidates.name: PMP address check
operation_candidates.rationale: PMP modules perform range/match checks for requested address.

operation_candidates.name: CLINT AXI-Lite read/write
operation_candidates.rationale: AXI-Lite interface decodes CLINT reads and writes.

operation_candidates.name: fuse read
operation_candidates.rationale: Fuse request/address/data path can expose security-sensitive fuse values.

operation_candidates.name: peripheral reset control
operation_candidates.rationale: Reset controls change peripheral lifecycle state.

guard_candidates.name: privilege/access-control gate
guard_candidates.rationale: Access decision depends on privilege-indexed access-control bits.

guard_candidates.name: boot ROM request mediation
guard_candidates.rationale: Boot ROM request is explicitly gated by access-control state for current privilege.

guard_candidates.name: register lock control
guard_candidates.rationale: Lock state likely prevents writes to protected wrapper registers.

guard_candidates.name: accounting/access-control input to DMA wrapper
guard_candidates.rationale: Per-wrapper access-control input may mediate DMA register operations.

guard_candidates.name: PMP allow/match mediation
guard_candidates.rationale: PMP check result/abort state mediates memory operations including DMA.

guard_candidates.name: address-range match construction
guard_candidates.rationale: PMP entry computes address ranges/masks for access authorization.

guard_candidates.name: debug authentication/unlock guard
guard_candidates.rationale: Unlock state is a candidate guard for JTAG/DMI debug paths.

guard_candidates.name: debug/test mode guard state
guard_candidates.rationale: Debug/test modes can qualify privileged access or bypass normal mediation.

guard_candidates.name: AXI address decode/enable/write-enable
guard_candidates.rationale: Address and enable/write-enable mediate which register operation occurs.

guard_candidates.name: external write-enable flags
guard_candidates.rationale: Write-enable flags may qualify protected register updates.

guard_candidates.name: reset state guard/lifecycle control
guard_candidates.rationale: Reset inputs and local reset controls affect initialization/clearing of protected state.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: global reset lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.semantic: Initializes or clears control, access, DMA, and interface state.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: JTAG reset lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.semantic: Independent test/debug reset affects JTAG path state.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: JTAG unlock lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.semantic: Authentication-derived unlock state gates or enables debug access; set/clear conditions should be tracked.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: access-control derivation lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.semantic: Derived policy bits update combinationally from base access-control vector and special alias condition.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: boot ROM access use site
state_lifecycle_facts.semantic: Access-control state is consumed when forming boot ROM request.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: debug master disabled/tied-off lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.semantic: Debug master response path appears forced inactive/zero in visible scope, relevant to reachability of debug bus accesses.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: DMA configuration capture
state_lifecycle_facts.semantic: DMA input configuration is retained in internal registers before or during transfer.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: DMA status/result lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.semantic: Completion/valid state records or exposes transfer progress/result.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: DMA PMP check lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.semantic: PMP address/check counter/allow state evolves during DMA authorization and can abort transfer.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: DMA wrapper register lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.semantic: AXI-visible DMA control/status registers retain configuration and results across cycles until reset/update.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: peripheral reset lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.semantic: Individual reset controls may clear peripheral state; update and authorization should be tracked.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: PMP range computation lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.semantic: Configured PMP address values are transformed into match masks/base for use in authorization.

path_candidates.name: JTAG-to-DMI debug path
path_candidates.sink: debug module operations and debug outputs
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Debug/test subject may perform privileged read/write/control operations if authorized by unlock/debug guard.

path_candidates.name: debug module bus path
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Debug bus master/slave path can bypass normal CPU privilege unless mediated; visible master response tie-offs affect reachability.

path_candidates.name: NoC-to-peripheral MMIO paths
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Network/bus requesters can access crypto, DMA, reset, debug, boot ROM, CLINT/PLIC, and policy peripherals.

path_candidates.name: CPU privilege to boot ROM read path
path_candidates.source: CPU/NoC request accounted as rom_req_acct with priv_lvl_i
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Protected memory read path with explicit privilege/access-control mediation.

path_candidates.name: AXI-Lite to CLINT register path
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Software-visible interrupt/timer controls require address and privilege mediation.

path_candidates.name: AXI/NoC to DMA configuration path
path_candidates.source: AXI requester ariane_axi / DMA NoC endpoint
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Requester can configure DMA source/destination/length subject to lock/access-control guards.

path_candidates.name: DMA transfer authorization path
path_candidates.permission_relevance: DMA memory access should be mediated by PMP/range checks before transfer proceeds.

path_candidates.name: PMP check path
path_candidates.source: requested physical address addr_i plus PMP config
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Central address-range authorization decision for memory accesses.

path_candidates.name: fuse access path
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Potential exposure path for fuse data; expected guards include privilege/access-control/debug/test restrictions.

path_candidates.name: reset-control path
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Reset operations can clear or alter protected state and should be mediated.

path_candidates.name: policy-register path
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Writes to policy state change future access decisions and are protected operations.

summary: Semantic labels identify access-control/lock policy state, debug/JTAG/DMI interfaces, DMA configuration and PMP mediation, boot ROM/fuse/reset/crypto peripheral assets, and candidate requester-to-asset paths. These labels are not vulnerability decisions.
